content.problemStatement:
Sasuke and Itachi are playing a game. Sasuke first creates an array AAA containing NNN positive integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. He then creates a new array BBB of length NNN such that Bi=gcd⁡(A1,A2,...,Ai)B_i = \gcd(A_1, A_2, ..., A_i)Bi​=gcd(A1​,A2​,...,Ai​) for each 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N. Now, Sasuke gives array BBB to Itachi and asks him to find any array AAA (with 1≤Ai≤1091\leq A_i\leq 10^91≤Ai​≤109) such that the given process applied to AAA will produce BBB. Can you help Itachi solve this problem?

Here, gcd⁡\gcdgcd stands for greatest common divisor.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers denoting the array AAA you constructed. If no such array AAA exists, print −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 5·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N. The second line contains N space‑separated integers B1,B2,…,BN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line. If a suitable array A exists, output N space‑separated integers A1,…,AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^9) that generate the given B. If no such array exists output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: B = [4,2] satisfies 4%2=0, so A can be [4,2]. Test case 2: 1%3≠0, impossible, output -1.

solution.approach: For i = 1 we must have A1 = B1 because gcd(A1) = B1. For i > 1 we need gcd(Ai, B_{i-1}) = B_i. This is possible iff B_i divides B_{i-1}. If the divisibility condition holds for every i, we can simply choose Ai = B_i (which satisfies gcd(B_{i-1}, B_i) = B_i). If any B_i does not divide B_{i-1}, the required array does not exist. The algorithm therefore scans the array once, checks the divisibility condition and either prints the B array itself or -1.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(sum of N) ≤ 5·10^5.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the input array of each test case.
